message:
fix(cancel): unbreak lib build for downstream crates

`pub mod protocol_validator_test_suite` was declared unconditionally at the
module root in `src/cancel/mod.rs`, but its source references a
pre-refactor snapshot of the validator API — patterns on removed enum
shapes (`TransitionResult::Invalid`, `ObligationEvent::Abort`,
`RegionState::Active`), fields that no longer exist on `RegionContext` /
`TaskContext` / `ObligationContext`, and removed `track_*` / `validate_*`
methods on `CancelProtocolValidator`. The module's re-exports were already
`#[cfg(test)]`-gated, but the `pub mod` line was not, so any downstream
crate depending on asupersync got ~105 compile errors before their own
code was touched.

- Gate `pub mod protocol_validator_test_suite` behind `#[cfg(test)]` so
  the stale harness only compiles under `cargo test` inside asupersync
  itself. Leave rewriting it to match the current validator API as a
  separate task.
- With the harness gated out, six state-machine structs
  (`RegionStateMachine`, `TaskStateMachine`, `ObligationStateMachine`,
  `ChannelStateMachine`, `IoStateMachine`, `TimerStateMachine`) have
  identity fields (`region_id`, `task_id`, `validation_level`, etc.)
  that are held by value but not read outside the now-gated harness.
  Under the crate-wide `#![deny(dead_code)]` this becomes a hard error.
  Attach `#[allow(dead_code)]` to each of the six structs, with a
  comment noting the fields are retained for tracing / external
  validator harnesses.
- Same fix for `GlobalEpochCounter::{last_advance, advance_interval}`
  in `src/runtime/epoch_tracking.rs:97`: rate-limit state that will be
  consumed by the advancement policy but currently lives unread.

Net effect: `cargo check --lib --features test-internals,tls-native-roots`
now succeeds on main. No public API changes; no behavioural changes.
Downstream crates (cass) can bump their asupersync pin to this commit
without the pre-refactor harness blowing up their builds.

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,15 @@
 
 pub mod progress_certificate;
 pub mod protocol_state_machines;
+// `protocol_validator_test_suite` references a pre-refactor snapshot of
+// `protocol_state_machines` (pattern-matches old enum shapes, calls removed
+// `track_*`/`validate_*` methods, constructs `ObligationContext`/`TaskContext`/
+// `RegionContext` with fields that no longer exist). Its re-exports in this
+// module are already `#[cfg(test)]`-gated, but the `pub mod` line was not —
+// so downstream crates would fail to compile the module against the current
+// API even when consuming asupersync as a library. Gate the module declaration
+// itself until the test harness is rewritten against the new validator API.
+#[cfg(test)]
 pub mod protocol_validator_test_suite;
 pub mod symbol_cancel;
